content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN elements. For any ordered triplet (i,j,k)(i, j, k)(i,j,k) such that iii, jjj, and kkk are pairwise distinct and 1≤i,j,k≤N1 \le i, j, k \le N1≤i,j,k≤N, the value of this triplet is (Ai−Aj)⋅Ak(A_i-A_j)\cdot A_k(Ai​−Aj​)⋅Ak​. You need to find the **maximum** value among all possible ordered triplets.

**Note:** Two ordered triplets (a,b,c)(a, b, c)(a,b,c) and (d,e,f)(d, e, f)(d,e,f) are only equal when a=da = da=d **and** b=eb = eb=e **and** c=fc = fc=f. As an example, (1,2,3)(1, 2, 3)(1,2,3) and (2,3,1)(2, 3, 1)(2,3,1) are two different ordered triplets.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. The test cases then follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, output the maximum value among all different ordered triplets.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all testcases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.
**Subtask 111 (30 points)**: Sum of NNN over all cases won't exceed 200020002000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The desired triplet is (3,2,1)(3, 2, 1)(3,2,1), which yields the value of (A3−A2)⋅A1=(3−1)⋅1=2(A_3 - A_2) \cdot A_1 = (3 - 1) \cdot 1 = 2(A3​−A2​)⋅A1​=(3−1)⋅1=2.
Test case 222: The desired triplet is (2,4,3)(2, 4, 3)(2,4,3), which yields the value of (A2−A4)⋅A3=(4−1)⋅4=12(A_2 - A_4) \cdot A_3 = (4 - 1) \cdot 4 = 12(A2​−A4​)⋅A3​=(4−1)⋅4=12.
Test case 333: The desired triplet is (1,2,3)(1, 2, 3)(1,2,3), which yields the value of (A1−A2)⋅A3=(23−17)⋅21=126(A_1 - A_2) \cdot A_3 = (23 - 17) \cdot 21 = 126(A1​−A2​)⋅A3​=(23−17)⋅21=126.
